title: Legacy001 two-seed RealMLP average
summary: Add a second RealMLP with a different seed and average it with the parent RealMLP without stacking or calibration.
rationale: Neural tabular models can have useful seed variance. Before adding a new model family or logistic stacker, a minimal two-seed average tests whether the RealMLP predictions stabilize and improve by simple ensembling.
implementation_hint: Keep the parent pipeline intact, train the same RealMLP configuration with one additional random seed such as 829 on the same folds, produce OOF/test probabilities for both seeds, and average them 0.5/0.5. Do not add LightGBM, CatBoost, XGBoost, rank stacking, or calibration in this patch.
expected_effect: Improve OOF AUC and reduce prediction variance if the RealMLP baseline is seed-sensitive.
risk: Roughly doubles RealMLP runtime and may give little benefit if both seeds are highly correlated.
